words.example: We have a stand-up every morning at 9 AM.

words.meaning: Something that prevents you from making progress
words.example: I have a blocker — I can't access the staging server.

words.meaning: Brief report on what you are working on
words.example: Let me give you an update on the login feature.
The API integration is still in progress.
The unit tests are done and all passing.

speaking.title: 💬 Speaking — Your First Stand-Up
speaking.context: It is 9:00 AM. Your team is on a video call for the daily stand-up. Your tech lead asks: "Okay everyone, let's start. Who wants to go first?" You decide to share your update. Yesterday you fixed a CSS bug on the homepage. Today you will start working on the user profile page. You have no blockers.
speaking.prompt: How do you give your stand-up update? Speak for about 30 seconds covering: what you did yesterday, what you will do today, and any blockers.
speaking.sample_answer: Sure, I'll go first! Yesterday I fixed the CSS alignment bug on the homepage — it's now deployed to staging. Today I'm planning to start on the user profile page, specifically the avatar upload feature. No blockers on my end. That's it from me!

reading.title: 📖 Reading — The Art of the Stand-Up
reading.passage:
The daily stand-up is one of the most important rituals in agile software development. It is a short, focused meeting where each team member answers three questions: What did I do yesterday? What will I do today? Are there any blockers?

The key to a good stand-up is brevity. Each person should speak for no more than two minutes. The goal is not to have a deep technical discussion — those conversations should happen after the meeting with only the relevant people.

Good stand-ups keep the whole team aligned. When you hear that a teammate is working on something related to your task, you can coordinate immediately. Blockers get surfaced quickly so the team lead can help remove them.

Remember: the stand-up is for the team, not for the manager. Speak to your teammates, not just to your boss.
questions: What are the three questions each person answers in a stand-up?
How long should each person speak during a stand-up?
answers: What did I do yesterday, what will I do today, and are there any blockers?
No more than two minutes per person.

key_phrases.phrase: Let me give you an update

key_phrases.meaning: Tidak ada hambatan dari sisi saya

items.phrase: Yesterday I worked on... Today I'm planning to...
items.indonesian: Kemarin saya mengerjakan... Hari ini saya berencana untuk...

items.phrase: I'm blocked by... — could someone help me with this?
items.indonesian: Saya terhambat oleh... — bisakah seseorang membantu saya?

items.phrase: That's all from me. No blockers.
items.indonesian: Sekian dari saya. Tidak ada hambatan.
